basic_details.job_title: UP Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 180 Anganwadi Worker Posts in Meerut District
basic_details.short_information: The District Programme Officer (ICDS), Meerut has released the Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 notification for 180 Anganwadi Worker (Karyakatri) vacancies across 13 Child Development Projects in urban and rural areas of Meerut district. Only female candidates aged 18 to 35 years as on 01 January 2026 who are permanent residents of the concerned gram sabha/ward are eligible to apply. Applications will be accepted online at http://upanganwadibharti.in from 27 June 2026 to 17 July 2026 (midnight 12:00).

application_fee.details: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for the Meerut UP Anganwadi Recruitment 2026. The online application at upanganwadibharti.in is free of cost for all eligible female candidates.

age_limit.details: Candidates wondering about the age requirement for the UP Anganwadi Meerut Recruitment 2026 should note that the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 35 years as on 01 January 2026 . Age will be verified only from the date of birth recorded in the High School (10th class) mark sheet and certificate. Candidates who are below 18 years or above 35 years as on 01 January 2026 are not eligible. | Post Minimum Age Maximum Age Age Cut-off Date Anganwadi Worker (Karyakatri) 18 years 35 years 01 January 2026

qualification.details: Only female candidates are eligible to apply for the Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026. The candidate must be a permanent resident of the gram sabha/ward (urban area) for which the vacancy exists. Applications from candidates who are residents of a different gram sabha/ward will not be accepted. | Candidates wondering about the age requirement for the UP Anganwadi Meerut Recruitment 2026 should note that the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 35 years as on 01 January 2026. Age will be verified only from the date of birth recorded in the High School (10th class) mark sheet and certificate. Candidates who are below 18 years or above 35 years as on 01 January 2026 are not eligible. | The applicant must be a permanent resident of the gram sabha or ward (in urban/shahari areas) for which the Anganwadi Worker vacancy exists. Applications from candidates residing in a different gram sabha or ward will not be accepted under any circumstances. This residential requirement is strictly enforced for the Meerut Anganwadi Vacancy 2026.

selection_process: There is no written examination for the Meerut Anganwadi Karyakatri Recruitment 2026. Selection will be conducted by the district-level Selection Committee (Chayan Samiti) constituted in Meerut district as per Government Order No. 1/1092245/2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025. The decision of the district-level Chayan Samiti will be final and binding.
Online applications are submitted through upanganwadibharti.in within the prescribed dates. Eligible candidates' details are verified for residential status

and qualification. A merit list is prepared on the basis of maximum educational qualification of the applicant. Graduates will be ranked above Intermediate pass candidates. Priority is given in the following order: BPL widows/divorced/abandoned women from the same gram sabha/ward → BPL women from the same gram sabha/ward → APL widows/divorced/abandoned women → other APL women from the same gram sabha/ward. Final selection is made by the district Chayan Samiti. The decision of the Chayan Samiti is final.
how_to_apply: Eligible female candidates can apply for the Meerut Anganwadi Worker Vacancy 2026 exclusively through the online portal at http://upanganwadibharti.in. The application portal is open from 27 June 2026 to 17 July 2026 (midnight 12:00). No offline applications sent by post or submitted in person will be accepted or considered under any circumstances for the Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026.
Visit the official UP Anganwadi recruitment portal at http://upanganwadibharti.in.
Check the gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy list for Meerut district to identify the specific post in your gram sabha or ward.
Register on the portal and fill in the online application form with your personal, educational, and residential details.

Upload self-attested copies of required documents - educational certificates, age proof (High School marksheet), residence certificate, income certificate, and caste certificate (if applicable) - all with online-verifiable digital signatures from the tehsil authority.
Submit the application form online before 17 July 2026 (midnight 12:00).
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Notification – PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: The official notification for the Meerut UP Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 has been issued by the District Programme Officer (ICDS), Meerut and is available on the official departmental website. The gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy details with category reservations will be uploaded on the official website http://upanganwadibharti.in as well as at the Collectorate, Vikas Bhavan, Tehsil, and Vikas Khand offices in Meerut. Candidates should download and refer to the gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy list before applying to ensure they are applying for the correct AWC in their area.

rows.document: Official Notification & Gram Sabha/Ward-wise Vacancy Details

content_sections.heading: Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026 – Quick Summary
content_sections.text: The UP Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Meerut notification has been issued by the District Programme Officer (ICDS) under Government Order No. 1/1092245/2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025. The recruitment covers 180 Anganwadi Karyakatri (Worker) vacancies across both urban and rural Child Development Projects in Meerut district.

content_sections.heading: Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for the Meerut Anganwadi Worker Vacancy 2026 must carefully note the application dates. The online application starts on 27 June 2026 and the last date to apply online is 17 July 2026 up to midnight (12:00) . No offline applications sent by post or delivered in person will be accepted under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: Meerut Anganwadi Vacancy 2026 – Project-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The Meerut Anganwadi Karyakatri Bharti 2026 covers vacancies across 13 Child Development Projects in Meerut district, including both urban (Shahari) and rural (Gramin) areas. A total of 180 Anganwadi Worker posts are available, with the number varying across projects. The gram sabha/ward-wise details with category reservations are available on the official website upanganwadibharti.in.

Rural + Urban (Shahjahanpur Nagar Panchayat – Ward No. 6, 7)
Rural + Urban (Daurala Nagar Panchayat – Ward No. 6, 7, 8, 9)
Rural + Urban (Hara Nagar Panchayat – Ward No. 6, 15)

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria – Meerut Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Only female candidates are eligible to apply for the Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026. The candidate must be a permanent resident of the gram sabha/ward (urban area) for which the vacancy exists. Applications from candidates who are residents of a different gram sabha/ward will not be accepted. Candidates wondering about the age requirement for the UP Anganwadi Meerut Recruitment 2026 should note that the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 35 years as on 01 January 2026 . Age will be verified only from the date of birth recorded in the High School (10th class) mark sheet and certificate. Candidates who are below 18 years or above 35 years as on 01 January 2026 are not eligible. The applicant must be a permanent resident of the gram sabha or ward (in urban/shahari areas) for which the Anganwadi Worker vacancy exists. Applications from candidates residing in a different gram sabha or ward will not be accepted under any circumstances. This residential requirement is strictly enforced for the Meerut Anganwadi Vacancy 2026.

rows.merit_basis: Merit prepared on the basis of maximum (highest) educational qualification. Candidates with higher qualifications such as Graduation (Parasnaatak) will be ranked above those with only Intermediate.

tables.caption: minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 35 years as on 01 January 2026

content_sections.heading: Application Fee – Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026
content_sections.text: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for the Meerut UP Anganwadi Recruitment 2026. The online application at upanganwadibharti.in is free of cost for all eligible female candidates.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process – Meerut Anganwadi Worker Vacancy 2026
content_sections.text: There is no written examination for the Meerut Anganwadi Karyakatri Recruitment 2026. Selection will be conducted by the district-level Selection Committee (Chayan Samiti) constituted in Meerut district as per Government Order No. 1/1092245/2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025. The decision of the district-level Chayan Samiti will be final and binding. Online applications are submitted through upanganwadibharti.in within the prescribed dates. Eligible candidates' details are verified for residential status, age, and qualification. A merit list is prepared on the basis of maximum educational qualification of the applicant. Graduates will be ranked above Intermediate pass candidates. Priority is given in the following order: BPL widows/divorced/abandoned women from the same gram sabha/ward → BPL women from the same gram sabha/ward → APL widows/divorced/abandoned women → other APL women from the same gram sabha/ward. Final selection is made by the district Chayan Samiti. The decision of the Chayan Samiti is final.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online
content_sections.text: Eligible female candidates can apply for the Meerut Anganwadi Worker Vacancy 2026 exclusively through the online portal at http://upanganwadibharti.in . The application portal is open from 27 June 2026 to 17 July 2026 (midnight 12:00) . No offline applications sent by post or submitted in person will be accepted or considered under any circumstances for the Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026. Visit the official UP Anganwadi recruitment portal at http://upanganwadibharti.in . Check the gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy list for Meerut district to identify the specific post in your gram sabha or ward. Register on the portal and fill in the online application form with your personal, educational, and residential details. Upload self-attested copies of required documents - educational certificates, age proof (High School marksheet), residence certificate, income certificate, and caste certificate (if applicable) - all with online-verifiable digital signatures from the tehsil authority. Submit the application form online before 17 July 2026 (midnight 12:00) . Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required – Meerut UP Anganwadi Bharti 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must upload readable and self-attested copies of all required documents on the online portal at the time of application. As per the official notification, income proof, residence, and caste certificates must be issued by the concerned tehsil authority with an online-verifiable digital signature. Documents that cannot be verified online will not be accepted. High School (10th class) marksheet and certificate - for age proof only. Intermediate (12th pass) marksheet and certificate - for educational qualification proof. Graduation certificate (if applicable) - for higher merit consideration. Permanent residence proof of the concerned gram sabha/ward - issued with tehsil-level online-verifiable digital signature. Caste certificate (if applicable) - issued with tehsil-level online-verifiable digital signature. Income certificate (BPL/APL classification) - issued as per UP Revenue Department Government Order No. 3959/1-9-11-Raj0-9 dated 30.09.2011 (as amended), with online-verifiable digital signature. Death certificate of husband (for widow applicants) - issued by gram panchayat/urban body AND family register authenticated copy. Court-issued legal order (for divorced/abandoned applicants).

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid in Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates make avoidable errors while applying for the Meerut Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026 that lead to rejection of applications. The points below are based on common patterns in similar UP government anganwadi drives and are not derived from the official notification. Applying for the wrong gram sabha/ward: Each Anganwadi Worker post is tied to a specific gram sabha or ward. Applying for a post outside your permanent residential area results in automatic disqualification. Submitting offline or postal applications: Only online applications through upanganwadibharti.in are accepted. Physically submitted or postal applications are completely invalid. Missing the midnight deadline on 17 July 2026: The portal closes at midnight (12:00) on 17 July 2026 - not at 5:00 PM. However, do not wait until the last day; technical issues near the deadline are common. Using age proof other than the High School marksheet: The official notification specifies that only the date of birth as recorded in the High School (Class X) marksheet and certificate will be accepted for age verification. Birth certificates or Aadhaar cards are not valid for this purpose. Uploading certificates without online-verifiable digital signatures: Caste, income, and residence certificates must be issued with a digital signature verifiable online. Manually signed certificates from the tehsil will not be accepted. Applying with incorrect or false information: If any error or false information is found in the application at any stage, the candidature will be cancelled and the candidate will be held responsible. No corrections will be allowed in offline mode after submission. Not checking the gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy list before applying: The total vacancy count may decrease due to special circumstances. Always verify the current vacancy status for your specific gram sabha/ward on upanganwadibharti.in before submitting.

faq.question: Q1. Has the Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 official notification been released?
faq.answer: Yes, the District Programme Officer (ICDS), Meerut has officially released the Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 notification on 25 June 2026 for 180 Anganwadi Worker vacancies across 13 Child Development Projects in Meerut district, Uttar Pradesh.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Meerut UP Anganwadi Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the Meerut Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026 is 17 July 2026 up to midnight (12:00). Applications are submitted only online at http://upanganwadibharti.in.

faq.question: Q3. How many total vacancies are available in Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 180 Anganwadi Worker (Karyakatri) vacancies are available across 13 Child Development Projects in Meerut district. The largest number of vacancies (56) is in the Shahar (Urban) project, followed by Daurala (18 rural + 4 urban) and Haastinapur (16 rural + 1 urban).

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Meerut Anganwadi Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 35 years as on 01 January 2026. Age will be verified only from the High School (Class X) marksheet and certificate. No other document will be accepted as age proof.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for Meerut Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualification is Intermediate (12th Pass) or equivalent. The merit list will be prepared on the basis of the maximum (highest) educational qualification. Candidates holding a graduation degree will be ranked above those with only Intermediate.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an application fee for Meerut Anganwadi Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification. The online application at upanganwadibharti.in is free of cost for all eligible female candidates.

faq.question: Q7. Is there a written exam for Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. There is no written examination. Selection is done by the district-level Chayan Samiti (Selection Committee) constituted in Meerut, based on merit from educational qualifications and priority category. The Chayan Samiti's decision is final.

faq.question: Q8. Can male candidates apply for Meerut Anganwadi Worker Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: No. The official notification clearly states that only female candidates are eligible to apply for the Anganwadi Worker posts in Meerut district.

faq.question: Q9. Can I apply for any project in Meerut or only for my own gram sabha/ward?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply only for the Anganwadi Worker post in their own gram sabha or ward where they are a permanent resident. Applications from candidates residing in a different gram sabha or ward will be rejected.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official website for Meerut Anganwadi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official website for submitting applications and downloading the gram sabha/ward-wise vacancy list is http://upanganwadibharti.in. Gram sabha/ward-wise details will also be available at the Collectorate, Vikas Bhavan, Tehsil, and Vikas Khand offices in Meerut.
